mission.title: Install and Configure Kubeflow on Kubernetes
mission.description: Kubeflow is a powerful Machine Learning Toolkit for Kubernetes, designed to support the entire AI lifecycle. Installing Kubeflow provides a modular, scalable, and extensible platform for AI practitioners and developers.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Kubeflow Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.
```bash
helm repo add kubeflow https://alauda.github.io/kubeflow-chart
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Kubeflow using Helm
steps.description:
Install Kubeflow in a new namespace called 'kubeflow'. This command will deploy the Kubeflow components.
```bash
helm install kubeflow kubeflow/kubeflow --namespace kubeflow --create-namespace --version 1.6.2
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check the status of the pods to ensure that all components are running.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace kubeflow
```

steps.title: Port forward to access the dashboard
steps.description:
Port forward the Kubeflow dashboard service to access it from your local machine.
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/istio-ingressgateway --namespace kubeflow 8080:80
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
Configure resource limits for the Kubeflow components to optimize performance.
```bash
kubectl patch deployment <deployment-name> -n kubeflow --type='json' -p='[{"op": "add", "path": "/spec/template/spec/containers/0/resources", "value": {"limits": {"cpu": "1000m", "memory": "2Gi"}, "requests": {"cpu": "500m", "memory": "1Gi"}}}]'
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Kubeflow release from your cluster.
```bash
helm uninstall kubeflow --namespace kubeflow
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and resources
uninstall.description:
Delete any remaining Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and other resources associated with Kubeflow.
```bash
kubectl delete crd $(kubectl get crd | grep kubeflow | awk '{print $1}')
```

uninstall.title: Remove the namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the 'kubeflow' namespace to clean up any remaining resources.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace kubeflow
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current state of your Kubeflow installation.
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace kubeflow -o yaml > kubeflow-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Update your Helm repositories to ensure you have the latest charts.
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kubeflow
upgrade.description:
Upgrade your existing Kubeflow installation to the latest version.
```bash
helm upgrade kubeflow kubeflow/kubeflow --namespace kubeflow --version 1.6.2
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check the status of the pods again to ensure that the upgrade was successful.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace kubeflow
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending state
troubleshooting.description:
If pods are stuck in Pending, check for available resources in your cluster.
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace kubeflow
kubectl get nodes
```

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If the Kubeflow dashboard is not reachable, ensure the port-forwarding command is running and check the service status.
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace kubeflow
```

troubleshooting.title: CrashLoopBackOff errors
troubleshooting.description:
If pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for errors.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace kubeflow
```

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient permissions
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter permission errors, ensure that your user has the necessary RBAC permissions.
```bash
kubectl auth can-i get pods --namespace kubeflow
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Kubeflow will have all pods in the 'kubeflow' namespace running without errors. You can access the Kubeflow dashboard through the port-forwarded URL.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add kubeflow https://alauda.github.io/kubeflow-chart
helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and Helm installed.
